Called TemplateManager for technical reasons (conflict with Kotlin typealias) 49→ * 50→ * This file contains the Kotlin portion of the template rendering code. 51→ * Templates can have filters applied to field replacements. 52→ * 53→ * The Rust template rendering code will apply any built in filters, and stop at the first 54→ * unrecognized filter. The remaining filters are returned to Kotlin, and applied using the hook system. 55→ * 56→ * For example, {{myfilter:hint:text:Field}} will apply the built in text and hint filters, 57→ * and then attempt to apply myfilter. If no add-ons have provided the filter, 58→ * the filter is skipped. 59→ */ 60→class TemplateManager { 61→ data class TemplateReplacement(val fieldName: String, var currentText: String, val filters: List) 62→ data class PartiallyRenderedCard(val qnodes: TemplateReplacementList, val anodes: TemplateReplacementList) { 63→ companion object { 64→ fun fromProto(out: anki.card_rendering.RenderCardResponse): PartiallyRenderedCard { 65→ val qnodes = nodesFromProto(out.questionNodesList) 66→ val anodes = nodesFromProto(out.answerNodesList) 67→ 68→ return PartiallyRenderedCard(qnodes, anodes) 69→ } 70→ 71→ fun nodesFromProto(nodes: List): TemplateReplacementList { 72→ val results: TemplateReplacementList = mutableListOf() 73→ for (node in nodes) { 74→ if (node.valueCase == anki.card_rendering.RenderedTemplateNode.ValueCase.TEXT) { 75→ results.append(Pair(node.text, null)) 76→ } else { 77→ results.append( 78→ Pair( 79→ null, 80→ TemplateReplacement( 81→ fieldName = node.replacement.fieldName, 82→ currentText = node.replacement.currentText, 83→ filters = node.replacement.filtersList 84→ ) 85→ ) 86→ ) 87→ } 88→ } 89→ 90→ return results 91→ } 92→ 93→ fun avTagToNative(tag: anki.card_rendering.AVTag): AvTag { 94→ val value = tag.valueCase 95→ return if (value == anki.card_rendering.AVTag.ValueCase.SOUND_OR_VIDEO) { 96→ SoundOrVideoTag(filename = tag.soundOrVideo) 97→ } else { 98→ TTSTag( 99→ fieldText = tag.tts.fieldText, 100→ lang = tag.tts.lang, 101→ voices = tag.tts.voicesList, 102→ otherArgs = tag.tts.otherArgsList, 103→ // The backend currently sends speed = 1, even when undefined. 104→ // We agreed that '1' should be classed as 'use system' and ignored 105→ // https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/issues/15598#issuecomment-1953653639 106→ speed = tag.tts.speed.let { if (it == 1f) null else it } 107→ ) 108→ } 109→ } 110→ 111→ fun avTagsToNative(tags: List): List { 112→ return tags.map { avTagToNative(it) }.toList() 113→ } 114→ } 115→ } 116→ 117→ /** 118→ * Holds information for the duration of one card render. 119→ * This may fetch information lazily in the future, so please avoid 120→ * using the _private fields directly. 121→ */ 122→ class TemplateRenderContext( 123→ card: Card, 124→ note: Note, 125→ browser: Boolean = false, 126→ notetype: NotetypeJson? = null, 127→ template: JSONObject? = null, 128→ private var fillEmpty: Boolean = false 129→ ) { 130→ private var _card: Card = card 131→ private var _note: Note = note 132→ private var _browser: Boolean = browser 133→ private var _template: JSONObject? = template 134→ 135→ private var noteType: NotetypeJson = notetype ?: note.notetype 136→ 137→ companion object { 138→ fun fromExistingCard(col: Collection, card: Card, browser: Boolean): TemplateRenderContext { 139→ return TemplateRenderContext(card, card.note(col), browser) 140→ } 141→ 142→ fun fromCardLayout( 143→ note: Note, 144→ card: Card, 145→ notetype: NotetypeJson, 146→ template: JSONObject, 147→ fillEmpty: Boolean 148→ ): TemplateRenderContext { 149→ return TemplateRenderContext( 150→ card, 151→ note, 152→ notetype = notetype, 153→ template = template, 154→ fillEmpty = fillEmpty 155→ ) 156→ } 157→ } 158→ 159→ /** 160→ * Returns the card being rendered. 161→ * Be careful not to call .q() or .a() on the card, or you'll create an 162→ * infinite loop. 163→ */ 164→ fun card() = _card 165→ 166→ fun note() = _note 167→ fun noteType() = noteType 168→ 169→ fun render(col: Collection): TemplateRenderOutput { 170→ val partial: PartiallyRenderedCard 171→ try { 172→ partial = partiallyRender(col) 173→ } catch (e: BackendTemplateException) { 174→ return TemplateRenderOutput( 175→ questionText = e.localizedMessage ?: e.toString(), 176→ answerText = e.localizedMessage ?: e.toString(), 177→ questionAvTags = emptyList(), 178→ answerAvTags = emptyList() 179→ ) 180→ } 181→ 182→ val mediaDir = col.media.dir 183→ val qtext = parseVideos( 184→ parseSourcesToFileScheme( 185→ applyCustomFilters(partial.qnodes, this, frontSide = null), 186→ mediaDir 187→ ), 188→ mediaDir 189→ ) 190→ val qout = col.backend.extractAvTags(text = qtext, questionSide = true) 191→ var qoutText = qout.text 192→ 193→ val atext = parseVideos( 194→ parseSourcesToFileScheme( 195→ applyCustomFilters(partial.anodes, this, frontSide = qout.text), 196→ mediaDir 197→ ), 198→ mediaDir 199→ ) 200→ val aout = col.backend.extractAvTags(text = atext, questionSide = false) 201→ var aoutText = aout.text 202→ 203→ if (!_browser) { 204→ val svg = noteType.optBoolean("latexsvg", false) 205→ qoutText = LaTeX.mungeQA(qoutText, col, svg) 206→ aoutText = LaTeX.mungeQA(aoutText, col, svg) 207→ } 208→ 209→ return TemplateRenderOutput( 210→ questionText = qoutText, 211→ answerText = aoutText, 212→ questionAvTags = avTagsToNative(qout.avTagsList), 213→ answerAvTags = avTagsToNative(aout.avTagsList), 214→ css = noteType().getString("css") 215→ ) 216→ } 217→ 218→ fun partiallyRender(col: Collection): PartiallyRenderedCard { 219→ val proto = col.run { 220→ if (_template != null) { 221→ // card layout screen 222→ backend.renderUncommittedCardLegacy( 223→ _note.toBackendNote(), 224→ _card.ord, 225→ BackendUtils.to_json_bytes(_template!!.deepClone()), 226→ fillEmpty, 227→ true 228→ ) 229→ } else { 230→ // existing card (eg study mode) 231→ backend.renderExistingCard(_card.id, _browser, true) 232→ } 233→ } 234→ return PartiallyRenderedCard.fromProto(proto) 235→ } 236→ 237→ /** Stores the rendered templates and extracted AV tags. */ 238→ data class TemplateRenderOutput( 239→ var questionText: String, 240→ var answerText: String, 241→ val questionAvTags: List, 242→ val answerAvTags: List, 243→ val css: String = "" 244→ ) { 245→ 246→ fun questionAndStyle() = "$questionText" 247→ fun answerAndStyle() = "$answerText" 248→ } 249→ 250→ /** Complete rendering by applying any pending custom filters. */ 251→ fun applyCustomFilters( 252→ rendered: TemplateReplacementList, 253→ ctx: TemplateRenderContext, 254→ frontSide: String? 255→ ): String { 256→ // template already fully rendered? 257→ if (len(rendered) == 1 && rendered[0].first != null) { 258→ return rendered[0].first!! 259→ } 260→ 261→ var res = "" 262→ for (union in rendered) { 263→ if (union.first != null) { 264→ res += union.first!! 265→ } else { 266→ val node = union.second!! 267→ // do we need to inject in FrontSide? 268→ if (node.fieldName == "FrontSide" && frontSide != null) { 269→ node.currentText = frontSide 270→ } 271→ 272→ var fieldText = node.currentText 273→ for (filterName in node.filters) { 274→ fieldFilters[filterName]?.let { 275→ fieldText = it.apply(fieldText, node.fieldName, filterName, ctx) 276→ } 277→ } 278→ 279→ res += fieldText 280→ } 281→ } 282→ return res 283→ } 284→ } 285→ 286→ /** 287→ * Defines custom `{{filters:..}}` 288→ * 289→ * Custom filters can check `filterName` to decide whether it should modify 290→ * `fieldText` or not before returning it 291→ */ 292→ abstract class FieldFilter { 293→ abstract fun apply( 294→ fieldText: String, 295→ fieldName: String, 296→ filterName: String, 297→ ctx: TemplateRenderContext 298→ ): String 299→ } 300→ companion object { 301→ val fieldFilters: MutableMap = mutableMapOf() 302→ } 303→} 304→ 305→/** 306→ * The desktop version handles videos in an external player (mpv) 307→ * because of old webview codecs in python, and to allow extending the video player. 308→ * To simplify things and deliver a better result, 309→ * we use the webview player, like AnkiMobile does 310→ * 311→ * `file:///` is used to enable seeking the video 312→ */ 313→@NotInLibAnki 314→@VisibleForTesting 315→fun parseVideos(text: String, mediaDir: String): String { 316→ return SOUND_RE.replace(text) { match -> 317→ val fileName = match.groupValues[1] 318→ val extension = fileName.substringAfterLast(".", "") 319→ if (extension in VIDEO_EXTENSIONS) { 320→ val path = Paths.get(mediaDir, fileName).toString() 321→ val uri = getFileUri(path) 322→ 323→ {"file_path":"AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BugReproductionTest.kt","content":"package com.ichi2.libanki import org.junit.Assert.assertEquals import org.junit.Test import com.ichi2.libanki.TemplateManager.parseSourcesToFileScheme class BugReproductionTest { @Test fun `parseSourcesToFileScheme - mixed script`() { val input = """
• type 1 RTA (distal): Sx: nephrocalcinosis, renal stone Hx
 

• ECG

저칼륨혈증 Hypokalemia
• 정의: plasma K {{c1::< 3.5}}
• 증상: {{c1::피로, 근육통, 힘빠짐, 변비, 장폐색, 부정맥, 다음, 다뇨}}
• 검사: {{c1::ECG (ST depression)}}

감별
• {{c1::Urine K > 15
    ∘}} {{c1::Metabolic acidosis:}} {{c1::DKA, RTA(type 1, 2), acetazolamide
    ∘}} {{c1::Metabolic alkalosis:
        ▸}} {{c1::BP↑: 고알도스테론(레닌↓), 리들, 신혈관고혈압, 신실질질환, 갈색세포종, ACTH
        ▸}} {{c1::BP−: 구토, 이뇨제, 바터, 기텔만, 저마그네슘}}
• {{c1::Urine K < 15
    ∘}} {{c1::Acidosis:}} {{c1::설사, 완화제
    ∘}} {{c1::Alkalosis:}} {{c1::발한}}

Tx: {{c1::원인제거, 경구 칼륨, KCl IV + N/S
    ∘ IV KCl: 속도:}} {{c1::≤20meq/L/hr, 농도 ≤40meq/L, 용량 ≤200meq/L/d}} 저칼륨혈증 Hypokalemia
• 정의: plasma K [...]
• 증상: [...]
• 검사: [...]

감별

1→//noinspection MissingCopyrightHeader #8659
 2→package com.ichi2.anki
 3→
 4→import com.ichi2.libanki.TTSTag
 5→import com.ichi2.libanki.template.TemplateFilters
 6→import org.jsoup.Jsoup
 7→import org.jsoup.nodes.Element
 8→import java.util.*
 9→
10→/**
11→ * Parse card sides, extracting text snippets that should be read using a text-to-speech engine.
12→ */
13→object TtsParser {
14→    /**
15→     * Returns the list of text snippets contained in the given HTML fragment that should be read
16→     * using the Android text-to-speech engine, together with the languages they are in.
17→     *
18→     *
19→     * Each returned LocalisedText object contains the text extracted from a `<tts>` element
20→     * whose 'service' attribute is set to 'android', and the localeCode taken from the 'voice'
21→     * attribute of that element. This holds unless the HTML fragment contains no such `<tts>`
22→     * elements; in that case the function returns a single LocalisedText object containing the
23→     * text extracted from the whole HTML fragment, with the localeCode set to an empty string.
24→     */
25→    fun getTextsToRead(html: String, clozeReplacement: String): List<TTSTag> {
26→        val textsToRead: MutableList<TTSTag> = ArrayList()
27→        val elem = Jsoup.parseBodyFragment(html).body()
28→        parseTtsElements(elem, textsToRead, clozeReplacement)
29→        if (textsToRead.isEmpty()) {
30→            // No <tts service="android"> elements found: return the text of the whole HTML fragment
31→            textsToRead.add(readWholeCard(elem.text().replace(TemplateFilters.CLOZE_DELETION_REPLACEMENT, clozeReplacement)))
32→        }
33→        return textsToRead
34→    }
35→
36→    private fun parseTtsElements(element: Element, textsToRead: MutableList<TTSTag>, clozeReplacement: String) {
37→        if ("tts".equals(element.tagName(), ignoreCase = true) &&
38→            "android".equals(element.attr("service"), ignoreCase = true)
39→        ) {
40→            textsToRead.add(localisedText(element.text().replace(TemplateFilters.CLOZE_DELETION_REPLACEMENT, clozeReplacement), element.attr("voice")))
41→            return // ignore any children
42→        }
43→        for (child in element.children()) {
44→            parseTtsElements(child, textsToRead, clozeReplacement)
45→        }
46→    }
47→
48→    /** If reading the whole card, a language cannot be determined */
49→    private fun readWholeCard(cardText: String) =
50→        localisedText(cardText, "")
51→
52→    private fun localisedText(text: String, locale: String): TTSTag =
53→        TTSTag(text, locale, emptyList(), 1.0f, emptyList())
54→}
55→

340→@NotInLibAnki
341→@VisibleForTesting
342→fun parseSourcesToFileScheme(content: String, mediaDir: String): String {
343→    val doc = Jsoup.parseBodyFragment(content)
344→    doc.outputSettings(OutputSettings().prettyPrint(false))
345→
346→    fun replaceWithFileScheme(tag: String, attr: String): Boolean {
347→        var madeChanges = false
348→        for (elem in doc.select(tag)) {
349→            val attrValue = elem.attr(attr)
350→            if (attrValue.isEmpty()) continue
351→
352→            val attrUri = try {
353→                URI(attrValue)
354→            } catch (_: URISyntaxException) {
355→                continue
356→            }
357→            if (attrUri.scheme != null) continue
358→
359→            val path = Paths.get(mediaDir, attrUri.path).toString()
360→            val newUri = getFileUri(path)
361→
362→            elem.attr(attr, newUri.toString())
363→            madeChanges = true
364→        }
365→        return madeChanges
366→    }
367→
368→    val hasMadeChanges =
369→        replaceWithFileScheme("img", "src") ||
370→            replaceWithFileScheme("video", "src") ||
371→            replaceWithFileScheme("audio", "src") ||
372→            replaceWithFileScheme("source", "src") ||
373→            replaceWithFileScheme("object", "data")
374→
375→    return if (hasMadeChanges) {
376→        doc.body().html()
377→    } else {
378→        content
379→    }

1→//noinspection MissingCopyrightHeader #8659
 2→package com.ichi2.anki
 3→
 4→import com.ichi2.libanki.TTSTag
 5→import com.ichi2.libanki.template.TemplateFilters
 6→import org.jsoup.Jsoup
 7→import org.jsoup.nodes.Element
 8→import java.util.*
 9→
10→/**
11→ * Parse card sides, extracting text snippets that should be read using a text-to-speech engine.
12→ */
13→object TtsParser {
14→    /**
15→     * Returns the list of text snippets contained in the given HTML fragment that should be read
16→     * using the Android text-to-speech engine, together with the languages they are in.
17→     *
18→     *
19→     * Each returned LocalisedText object contains the text extracted from a `<tts>` element
20→     * whose 'service' attribute is set to 'android', and the localeCode taken from the 'voice'
21→     * attribute of that element. This holds unless the HTML fragment contains no such `<tts>`
22→     * elements; in that case the function returns a single LocalisedText object containing the
23→     * text extracted from the whole HTML fragment, with the localeCode set to an empty string.
24→     */
25→    fun getTextsToRead(html: String, clozeReplacement: String): List<TTSTag> {
26→        val textsToRead: MutableList<TTSTag> = ArrayList()
27→        val elem = Jsoup.parseBodyFragment(html).body()
28→        parseTtsElements(elem, textsToRead, clozeReplacement)
29→        if (textsToRead.isEmpty()) {
30→            // No <tts service="android"> elements found: return the text of the whole HTML fragment
31→            textsToRead.add(readWholeCard(elem.text().replace(TemplateFilters.CLOZE_DELETION_REPLACEMENT, clozeReplacement)))
32→        }
33→        return textsToRead
34→    }
35→
36→    private fun parseTtsElements(element: Element, textsToRead: MutableList<TTSTag>, clozeReplacement: String) {
37→        if ("tts".equals(element.tagName(), ignoreCase = true) &&
38→            "android".equals(element.attr("service"), ignoreCase = true)
39→        ) {
40→            textsToRead.add(localisedText(element.text().replace(TemplateFilters.CLOZE_DELETION_REPLACEMENT, clozeReplacement), element.attr("voice")))
41→            return // ignore any children
42→        }
43→        for (child in element.children()) {
44→            parseTtsElements(child, textsToRead, clozeReplacement)
45→        }
46→    }
47→
48→    /** If reading the whole card, a language cannot be determined */
49→    private fun readWholeCard(cardText: String) =
50→        localisedText(cardText, "")
51→
52→    private fun localisedText(text: String, locale: String): TTSTag =
53→        TTSTag(text, locale, emptyList(), 1.0f, emptyList())
54→}
55→

340→@NotInLibAnki
341→@VisibleForTesting
342→fun parseSourcesToFileScheme(content: String, mediaDir: String): String {
343→    val doc = Jsoup.parseBodyFragment(content)
344→    doc.outputSettings(OutputSettings().prettyPrint(false))
345→
346→    fun replaceWithFileScheme(tag: String, attr: String): Boolean {
347→        var madeChanges = false
348→        for (elem in doc.select(tag)) {
349→            val attrValue = elem.attr(attr)
350→            if (attrValue.isEmpty()) continue
351→
352→            val attrUri = try {
353→                URI(attrValue)
354→            } catch (_: URISyntaxException) {
355→                continue
356→            }
357→            if (attrUri.scheme != null) continue
358→
359→            val path = Paths.get(mediaDir, attrUri.path).toString()
360→            val newUri = getFileUri(path)
361→
362→            elem.attr(attr, newUri.toString())
363→            madeChanges = true
364→        }
365→        return madeChanges
366→    }
367→
368→    val hasMadeChanges =
369→        replaceWithFileScheme("img", "src") ||
370→            replaceWithFileScheme("video", "src") ||
371→            replaceWithFileScheme("audio", "src") ||
372→            replaceWithFileScheme("source", "src") ||
373→            replaceWithFileScheme("object", "data")
374→
375→    return if (hasMadeChanges) {
376→        doc.body().html()
377→    } else {
378→        content
379→    }
